pattern: A grid graph's corner (the only degree-2 nodes when both dimensions exceed one) bootstraps two perpendicular walks, and each later row is then read off the previous two rows by elimination.
hints: If every node has degree at most 2, the whole thing is really one row (or column) - just walk the path from an endpoint. Otherwise only true corners have degree exactly 2.

Anchor on one corner and its two neighbours - one becomes "next in this row", the other "next in this column". The cell diagonally next to the corner is the one common neighbour those two share (besides the corner itself).

Grow the first row one cell at a time: at each row-0 cell, the neighbour that ISN'T the previous row-0 cell and ISN'T the cell directly below it must be the next row-0 cell (none left means the row is done). Once two full rows are known, every later row follows the same elimination against the two rows above it.

edge_inputs.description: two nodes, the smallest possible grid (a single row of two)
a 2x2 grid whose node ids are assigned so the diagonal pairs are not the obvious 0-3/1-2 split
a 2-row by 3-column rectangle with row-major ids
a 3x3 grid, the smallest layout with a true interior node of degree 4
a single-row layout whose node ids are a shuffled, non-monotonic walk order
a tall 4-row by 2-column rectangle, forcing several rows to be derived beyond the first two
a wide single-column layout (the transpose of a row) to confirm orientation does not matter

stress.description: a randomly shaped rectangle near the node-count limit with ids shuffled across the grid positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total = max(4, min(size, 50000))
    rows = max(2, int(total ** 0.5))
    cols = max(2, total // rows)
    n = rows * cols
    ids = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(ids)
    grid = [ids[r * cols:(r + 1) * cols] for r in range(rows)]
    edges = []
    for r in range(rows):
        for c in range(cols):
            if c + 1 < cols:
                a, b = grid[r][c], grid[r][c + 1]
                edges.append([min(a, b), max(a, b)])
            if r + 1 < rows:
                a, b = grid[r][c], grid[r + 1][c]
                edges.append([min(a, b), max(a, b)])
    return [n, edges]
stress.note: shuffling ids across grid positions means no row/column is ever in id order, matching real inputs

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def constructGridLayout(self, n, edges):
        if n == 1:
            return [[0]]
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        deg = [len(a) for a in adj]

        if max(deg) <= 2 and any(d == 1 for d in deg):
            start = min(i for i in range(n) if deg[i] == 1)
            path = [start]
            prev, cur = None, start
            while len(path) < n:
                nxt = next(v for v in adj[cur] if v != prev)
                path.append(nxt)
                prev, cur = cur, nxt
            return [path]

        corner = min(i for i in range(n) if deg[i] == 2)
        a, b = sorted(adj[corner])

        row0 = [corner, a]
        row1 = [b, None]
        prev0, prevRef, cur0 = corner, b, a
        while True:
            common = (set(adj[cur0]) & set(adj[prevRef])) - {prev0}
            below = next(iter(common)) if common else None
            row1[-1] = below
            exclude = {prev0} | ({below} if below is not None else set())
            nxt_candidates = [v for v in adj[cur0] if v not in exclude]
            if not nxt_candidates:
                break
            nxt = nxt_candidates[0]
            row0.append(nxt)
            row1.append(None)
            prev0, prevRef, cur0 = cur0, below, nxt

        cols = len(row0)
        grid = [row0, row1]
        while len(grid) * cols < n:
            prev_row, last_row = grid[-2], grid[-1]
            next_row = []
            ok = True
            for j in range(cols):
                exclude = {prev_row[j]}
                if j > 0:
                    exclude.add(last_row[j - 1])
                if j < cols - 1:
                    exclude.add(last_row[j + 1])
                cands = [v for v in adj[last_row[j]] if v not in exclude]
                if not cands:
                    ok = False
                    break
                next_row.append(cands[0])
            if not ok:
                break
            grid.append(next_row)
        return grid
